(German pronunciation: [[ˈmɪlbɐtsˌhoːfn̩]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Standard_German "Help:IPA/Standard German") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:LL-Q188_(deu)-Ionenlaser-Milbertshofen.wav "File:LL-Q188 (deu)-Ionenlaser-Milbertshofen.wav"); [Central Bavarian](/wiki/Central_Bavarian "Central Bavarian"): *Muibatshofa*), **[Am Riesenfeld](/wiki/Am_Riesenfeld "Am Riesenfeld")** and **[Am Hart](/wiki/Am_Hart "Am Hart")** (German pronunciation: [[am ˈhaʁt]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Standard_German "Help:IPA/Standard German") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:LL-Q188_(deu)-Ionenlaser-Am_Hart.wav "File:LL-Q188 (deu)-Ionenlaser-Am Hart.wav"); [Central Bavarian](/wiki/Central_Bavarian "Central Bavarian"): *Am Hoart*) are three [boroughs](/wiki/Borough "Borough") situated in the north of [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich") in [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"). Jointly, they form the city district **11 Milbertshofen-Am Hart**. As of December 2016[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Milbertshofen-Am_Hart&action=edit), the three boroughs had 76,255 inhabitants.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Borough of Munich, Germany

| --- | --- |

Unsourced material may be challenged and [removed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability#Burden_of_evidence "Wikipedia:Verifiability"). *Find sources:* ["Milbertshofen-Am Hart"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Milbertshofen-Am+Hart%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Milbertshofen-Am+Hart%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Milbertshofen-Am+Hart%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Milbertshofen-Am+Hart%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Milbertshofen-Am+Hart%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Milbertshofen-Am+Hart%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(March 2026)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/57/Munich_-_Olympiapark_3.jpg/250px-Munich_-_Olympiapark_3.jpg)](/wiki/File:Munich_-_Olympiapark_3.jpg)

[Olympiahalle](/wiki/Olympiahalle "Olympiahalle"), [Olympic Stadium](/wiki/Olympic_Stadium,_Munich "Olympic Stadium, Munich") and [Olympic Swim Hall](/wiki/Olympia_Schwimmhalle "Olympia Schwimmhalle")

**Milbertshofen** (German pronunciation: [[ˈmɪlbɐtsˌhoːfn̩]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Standard_German "Help:IPA/Standard German") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:LL-Q188_(deu)-Ionenlaser-Milbertshofen.wav "File:LL-Q188 (deu)-Ionenlaser-Milbertshofen.wav"); [Central Bavarian](/wiki/Central_Bavarian "Central Bavarian"): *Muibatshofa*), **[Am Riesenfeld](/wiki/Am_Riesenfeld "Am Riesenfeld")** and **[Am Hart](/wiki/Am_Hart "Am Hart")** (German pronunciation: [[am ˈhaʁt]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Standard_German "Help:IPA/Standard German") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:LL-Q188_(deu)-Ionenlaser-Am_Hart.wav "File:LL-Q188 (deu)-Ionenlaser-Am Hart.wav"); [Central Bavarian](/wiki/Central_Bavarian "Central Bavarian"): *Am Hoart*) are three [boroughs](/wiki/Borough "Borough") situated in the north of [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich") in [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"). Jointly, they form the city district **11 Milbertshofen-Am Hart**. As of December 2016[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Milbertshofen-Am_Hart&action=edit), the three boroughs had 76,255 inhabitants.

## Location

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Milbertshofen-Am_Hart&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Location")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/39/M%C3%BCnchen_-_Stadtbezirk_11_%28Karte%29_-_Milbertshofen_-_Am_Hart.png/250px-M%C3%BCnchen_-_Stadtbezirk_11_%28Karte%29_-_Milbertshofen_-_Am_Hart.png)](/wiki/File:M%C3%BCnchen_-_Stadtbezirk_11_(Karte)_-_Milbertshofen_-_Am_Hart.png)

Milbertshofen-Am Hart on the map of city districts

Milbertshofen-Am Hart is surrounded by [Schwabing-Freimann](/wiki/Schwabing-Freimann "Schwabing-Freimann") (east), [Schwabing-West](/wiki/Schwabing-West "Schwabing-West") (south), [Neuhausen-Nymphenburg](/wiki/Neuhausen-Nymphenburg "Neuhausen-Nymphenburg") (southwest), [Moosach](/wiki/Moosach_(M%C3%BCnchen) "Moosach (München)") and [Feldmoching-Hasenbergl](/wiki/Feldmoching-Hasenbergl "Feldmoching-Hasenbergl") (west). North of it comes the municipality [Oberschleißheim](/wiki/Oberschlei%C3%9Fheim "Oberschleißheim").

## Description

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Milbertshofen-Am_Hart&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Description")]

In Milbertshofen-Am Hart is the [Olympiapark](/wiki/Olympiapark_(Munich) "Olympiapark (Munich)") (with the 291-metre-high [Olympiaturm](/wiki/Olympiaturm "Olympiaturm") Munich's tallest building, the [Olympiahalle](/wiki/Olympiahalle "Olympiahalle"), [Olympiastadion](/wiki/Olympiastadion_(Munich) "Olympiastadion (Munich)"), [Erinnerungsort Olympia-Attentat](/wiki/Erinnerungsort_Olympia-Attentat "Erinnerungsort Olympia-Attentat"), [Sea Life München](/wiki/Sea_Life_Centres "Sea Life Centres"), [Olympic Village](/wiki/Olympic_Village,_Munich "Olympic Village, Munich")), and the BMW Museums ([BMW Welt](/wiki/BMW_Welt "BMW Welt"), [BMW Group Classic](/wiki/BMW_Group_Classic "BMW Group Classic"), [BMW Museum](/wiki/BMW_Museum "BMW Museum") and [BMW Tower](/wiki/BMW_Headquarters "BMW Headquarters")). Other notable buildings include [BMW FIZ](/wiki/BMW_FIZ "BMW FIZ"), [Knorr-Bremse](/wiki/Knorr-Bremse "Knorr-Bremse") headquarter, [Bayerisches Landesamt für Verfassungsschutz](/wiki/Bayerisches_Landesamt_f%C3%BCr_Verfassungsschutz "Bayerisches Landesamt für Verfassungsschutz"), Euro-Industriepark, [Mira shopping center](/wiki/Mira_(Shopping_center) "Mira (Shopping center)") and [Gymnasium München Nord](/wiki/Gymnasium_M%C3%BCnchen_Nord "Gymnasium München Nord"). Green Areas include: [Petuelpark](/wiki/Petuelpark "Petuelpark"), [Hartelholz](/wiki/Hartelholz "Hartelholz") and [Panzerwiese](/wiki/Panzerwiese "Panzerwiese"). The Olympiapark is the location of the [Zentrale Hochschulsportanlage](/wiki/Zentrale_Hochschulsportanlage "Zentrale Hochschulsportanlage"), [Spartan Race](/wiki/Spartan_Race "Spartan Race") Sprint as well as Start- and Finish location of the [Munich Marathon](/wiki/Munich_Marathon "Munich Marathon") and Start of the [Wings for Life World Run Munich](/wiki/Wings_for_Life_World_Run "Wings for Life World Run").

[![logo](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/4/4a/Commons-logo.svg/40px-Commons-logo.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Commons-logo.svg)
